steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Label the side pair for the named angle first: opposite, adjacent, and hypotenuse. Then pick the matching shortcut, such as SOH/CAH/TOA, complementary trig values, or a special right-triangle ratio.

steps.title: Set up the tangent ratio
steps.content:
In right triangle LMN with the right angle at N, angle L is one of the acute angles. From angle L's perspective:
  • Opposite side: $MN$
  • Adjacent side: $LN$
Therefore:
$$\tan L = \frac{MN}{LN} = \frac{9}{40}$$

steps.title: Find LN, then use the Pythagorean triple
steps.content:
Since $MN = 27$ and $\frac{MN}{LN} = \frac{9}{40}$:
$$\frac{27}{LN} = \frac{9}{40} \implies LN = \frac{27 \cdot 40}{9} = 120$$
The legs are now in ratio $27: 120 = 9: 40$. This is the 9-40-41 Pythagorean triple. With scale factor $k = 3$, the hypotenuse is $LM = 41 \cdot 3 = 123$.

steps.title: Calculate the perimeter
steps.content:
Adding all three sides:
$$120 + 27 + 123 = 270$$
That confirms B is correct.
